title: End of module: Exponentials and Logarithms

questions.text: For $y = a^x$ with $0 < a < 1$, which statement correctly describes the graph?
choices: It is increasing and passes through $(0,1)$
It is decreasing and passes through $(0,1)$
It is decreasing and passes through $(1,0)$
It is a straight line through the origin
questions.explanation: When $0<a<1$, $a^x$ decreases as $x$ increases (exponential decay), and every curve $y=a^x$ passes through $(0,1)$ since $a^0=1$.

questions.text: Which expression is equivalent to $\log_a(x) = y$?
questions.explanation: By definition, a logarithm is the inverse of an exponential: $\log_a(x) = y$ means precisely $a^y = x$.
$64 = 2^6$, so by the definition of a logarithm $\log_2(64) = \log_2(2^6) = 6$.

questions.text: Solve $5^x = 30$ for $x$, giving your answer to 3 decimal places.

questions.explanation: $\ln$ and $e^{(\cdot)}$ are inverse functions, so $\ln(e^3) = 3$ directly (or: $\ln(e^3) = 3\ln e = 3 \times 1 = 3$).

questions.text: Which of the following correctly applies the laws of logarithms to write $\log_a A + \log_a B - \log_a C$ as a single logarithm?
questions.explanation: The product law combines $\log_a A + \log_a B$ into $\log_a(AB)$; the quotient law then subtracts $\log_a C$ to give $\log_a\left(\dfrac{AB}{C}\right)$.

questions.text: Select all statements that are correct applications of the laws of logarithms (for valid, positive arguments).
questions.explanation: The power law gives $\log_a(A^n)=n\log_a A$ (true) and the quotient law gives $\log_a A - \log_a B = \log_a(A/B)$ (true). $\log_a\left(\frac{1}{A}\right) = \log_a(A^{-1}) = -\log_a A$ (true, a special case of the power law). However $\log_a A + \log_a B = \log_a(AB)$, **not** $\log_a(A+B)$. Logs turn multiplication into addition, not addition into addition, so the third statement is false.

Combine with the product law: $\log_3\big((x+2)(x-2)\big) = 2$, so $(x+2)(x-2) = 3^2 = 9$. Then $x^2 - 4 = 9$, so $x^2 = 13$ and $x = \sqrt{13} \approx 3.606$ (the negative root $x=-\sqrt{13}$ is rejected since it fails $x>2$, which is needed for both logs to be defined).

questions.text: A population of bacteria grows according to $P = 50e^{0.08t}$, where $t$ is in hours. Find the population after 15 hours, to the nearest whole number.
A radioactive isotope decays according to $A = 200e^{-0.03t}$ (t in years). Find the half-life of the isotope, to 1 decimal place (the time for $A$ to fall to $100$).
\$1000 is invested at a continuously compounded annual rate of 5%, so the balance after $t$ years is $A = 1000e^{0.05t}$. Find the balance after 8 years, to the nearest penny.
questions.explanation: Substitute $t=8$: $A = 1000e^{0.05\times8} = 1000e^{0.4}$. Since $e^{0.4} = 1.491825\ldots$, $A = 1491.82$ to the nearest penny.

questions.text: An equation of the form $a^x = k$ (with $a>0$, $a\ne1$, and $k \le 0$) has:
questions.explanation: Since $a^x > 0$ for every real $x$ (an exponential function never reaches zero or goes negative), the equation $a^x = k$ has no real solution when $k \le 0$.

This one reaches back to fractional indices from the prerequisite module Indices and Surds: rewrite $8\sqrt{2}$ as a single power of 2 before applying the log. Since $8=2^3$ and $\sqrt{2}=2^{1/2}$, we get $8\sqrt{2}=2^3\times2^{1/2}=2^{3.5}$, so $\log_2(8\sqrt{2})=\log_2(2^{3.5})=3.5$. Spaced returns like this to earlier skills are what make them stick.
